suppress.notes: FALSE POSITIVE (verified 2026-06-11; pending Security-Lead confirmation). CVE-2026-42154 is an unauthenticated DoS in the PROMETHEUS SERVER's remote-read endpoint (/api/v1/read) — the Go project github.com/prometheus/prometheus, fixed in Prometheus 3.5.3 / 3.11.3 (ref: nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-42154). This service depends on io.micrometer:micrometer-registry-prometheus, the Java metrics-EXPOSITION client that serves /actuator/prometheus for scraping. It implements NO remote-read endpoint and contains none of the vulnerable Prometheus-server code. dependency-check mis-attributes the server CVE to the similarly-named client via fuzzy CPE matching; no micrometer release (incl. latest 1.17.0) will ever "fix" a Prometheus-server CVE. Scoped to this package + CVE only.

FALSE POSITIVE (verified 2026-06-15; pending Security-Lead confirmation). CVE-2026-39883 is a PATH-hijacking / untrusted-search-path flaw (CWE-426) in the OpenTelemetry-GO SDK's BSD `kenv` command execution — the Go module go.opentelemetry.io/otel, fixed in opentelemetry-go v1.43.0 (ref: GHSA-hfvc-g4fc-pqhx, nvd.nist.gov/vuln/detail/CVE-2026-39883). This service depends on the JAVA artifact io.opentelemetry.semconv:opentelemetry-semconv (semantic-convention constants only — pulled transitively via micrometer-tracing-bridge-otel / opentelemetry-exporter-otlp). It contains NO Go code and none of the affected kenv/exec path-resolution logic. dependency-check mis-attributes the Go-SDK CVE to the similarly-named Java jar via fuzzy CPE matching; no opentelemetry-semconv (Java) release will ever "fix" a Go-SDK CVE. Scoped to this package + CVE only.
